acceptanceCriteria: A versioned outcome schema (rigor.outcome.v1) links task, routing plan, attempt, verification, review, and commit/PR when available.

Outcome records accepted/rejected status, accepted-without-model-code-changes, human correction minutes, retry/escalation count, review finding counts by severity, revert/escaped-defect status, and usage status.
Tokens, provider cost, model identity, and reasoning effort are represented as measured values or explicit unknown/unavailable values, never fabricated.
Schemas reject fabricated or internally inconsistent measurements.
Existing v0.2 artifacts remain readable (schema additions are backward compatible).
rigor retrospect reports per-candidate success rate, retry rate, elapsed time, human intervention, and data-completeness counts, with denominators and missing-data counts, not just totals.
Prose is redacted and secrets rejected consistently with existing artifact rules.
Tests cover accepted, rejected, reverted, and usage-unavailable outcomes.
Documentation states that relative cost is not a provider invoice.
constraints: Implement only the Scope and Acceptance criteria of issue #9; do not add unrelated features.
Do not add external transmission; no network calls.
Do not delete, weaken, or skip existing tests; new tests may be added.
Represent unavailable measurements explicitly as unknown/unavailable; never infer cost from a model display name.
Treat configured model identity as unverified; do not claim attestation.
Keep existing v0.2 artifacts readable; schema changes must be additive.
Bound and validate all prose with the existing textField/strings rules (non-empty, length-limited, no null bytes).
stopConditions: a required check fails twice without a new hypothesis
